scores.dimension: Layer coverage (unit / integration / fuzz / invariant / symbolic / fork)
scores.anchor_matched: 7 anchor: unit, integration, invariant, and fork layers are non-trivially populated; lower-anchor observations are 0 testFuzz_ functions and 0 symbolic/prove functions, so the suite cannot reach the 9 anchor.

evidence.note: Unit-style tests cover start/resolve success, revert, and event paths.
Integration tests exercise accepted, rejected, not-yet-resolved, and second-evaluation lifecycles.
Multi-contract phase-5 simulation tests legitimate and adversarial paths.
StdInvariant harness targets a handler and defines two invariant_* properties.
Gated Gnosis fork test reads live proposal/CTF state and asserts outcome/payout properties.
Observed counts: 250 test functions, 0 testFuzz_ functions, 5 invariant markers, 0 symbolic/prove functions, 8 fork calls; no Echidna/Medusa/Certora files outside excluded deps.

scores.anchor_matched: Between 5 and 7: stable spec IDs and a partial pass-status matrix exist, and source NatSpec cites IDs, but fewer than 60% of invariants map to executable tests and test files contain no INV-* or @spec tags.

evidence.note: Canonical invariant document defines stable IDs, prose, predicates, citations, and STATED/TESTED/PROVED status.
Traceability rules require test tags; pass-status table maps only a subset of 15 IDs to partial or named tests, leaving many untested.
Source NatSpec references INV-SALE-001, INV-SALE-004, INV-SALE-002, and INV-SALE-003.
Source NatSpec references INV-ARB-005, INV-ARB-004, INV-ARB-002, and INV-ARB-006.
No matches in test files, so executable checks are not tagged with spec IDs.

scores.anchor_matched: 3 band with upward adjustment: coverage is above 50% and tests are assertion-rich, but there is no mutation tool, no mutation baseline, no coverage gate, and coverage is below the 75%/gate requirements for the 5 anchor.

evidence.note: Total coverage row reported 59.45% lines, 57.37% statements, 31.73% branches, and 65.10% functions.
Representative behavior tests assert exact bond matching and revert conditions rather than only no-revert smoke.
Representative sale tests assert effective-supply and ragequit accounting state deltas.
Foundry config has no mutation, coverage threshold, or CI profile configuration.
CI runs fmt, build, and forge test only; no coverage or mutation gate is configured.
No vertigo/gambit/sumo/mutation config or .vertigorc/vertigo.yml was found in foundry.toml, .github, Makefile, or repo root.

scores.anchor_matched: 5 anchor: fork tests cover major external boundaries and are env-gated, but all observed forks use floating latest blocks and CI does not run fork tests; no Tenderly or mainnet-shadow staging is wired.

evidence.note: Gnosis fork test is gated by RUN_GNOSIS_FORK_TESTS and uses createSelectFork(vm.rpcUrl("gnosis")) with no pinned block.
Swapr/Algebra fork test exercises real Gnosis token and position-manager addresses, also with an unpinned fork.
E2E fork test documents RUN_GNOSIS_FORK_TESTS gating and forks Gnosis without a block number.
The main CI workflow runs forge test without setting RUN_GNOSIS_FORK_TESTS or a fork-specific job.
Observed 8 createSelectFork/createFork call sites and no .github workflow match for RUN_.*FORK.*TESTS or forge test.*fork.

scores.anchor_matched: Between 3 and 5: PR CI runs fmt/build/tests and a separate Slither gate exists, but Foundry is not version-pinned, [profile.ci] is absent, seeds are not deterministic, and coverage/gas/fork gates are missing.

evidence.note: CI runs on push, pull_request, and manual dispatch with FOUNDRY_PROFILE=ci.
Workflow checks out code, installs foundry-rs/foundry-toolchain@v1 without a version input, then runs fmt, build, and forge test.
Only default profile, lint, rpc_endpoints, and fmt sections exist; no [profile.ci], fuzz seed, invariant seed, or coverage/gas gates are defined.
Separate Slither job is configured to run on PR/push and fail on medium/high findings while uploading SARIF.
Search found no forge coverage, forge snapshot, gas-report, codecov, deterministic seed, fork-test job, or artifact upload in CI.

scores.anchor_matched: 3 anchor with slight upward adjustment for a blocking Slither workflow: the committed stack is Foundry plus Slither only; SMTChecker, Echidna/Medusa/Halmos/hevm/Certora/Kontrol, and differential tests are absent.

evidence.note: Slither action is wired with fail-on: medium and an explicit failure re-emission step.
Slither configuration excludes low-signal detectors/dependencies and forces Foundry compilation.
No SMTChecker/model_checker, symbolic, or external-fuzzer profile is configured.

Search excluding lib/.claude/out/cache found no echidna, medusa, halmos, certora, kontrol, hevm, smtchecker, or model_checker config; differential/equivalence search in test files returned no matches.

evaluator_notes: No prior topic-4 evaluation file existed, so no per-dimension deltas were computed. forge was not on PATH; commands used ~/.foundry/bin/forge. Non-fork forge test passed 245/245. Coverage total is Forge's reported total including test/mocks. Negative tool searches excluded lib/.claude/out/cache where noted.

scores.dimension: Layer coverage (unit / integration / fuzz / invariant / symbolic / fork)
scores.anchor_matched: Between 7 and 9: unit, integration, invariant, and fork layers are populated, and symbolic/SMT workflows are now wired in CI; lower-anchor observations remain 0 testFuzz_ functions, 0 testSym_/prove functions, and fork tests are not CI-executed with pinned blocks, so the suite cannot reach the 9 anchor.

evidence.note: Unit-style tests cover startEvaluation success, revert paths, oracle binding, and assertions.
Integration tests execute accepted, rejected, unresolved, and repeated lifecycle paths across arbitration + evaluation pipeline.
Multi-contract simulation exercises promotion, adversarial pre-creation attempts, TWAP programming, resolution, and report assertions.
StdInvariant suite targets a handler and defines three INV_* invariant functions for sale/token properties.
Existing StdInvariant suite targets arbitration handler calls and defines two conservation/accounting invariants.
Scheduled/manual/PR symbolic workflow runs solc SMTChecker and Halmos against INV_* invariant tests.
Observed counts: 251 test functions, 0 testFuzz_ functions, 11 invariant markers, 0 testSym_/prove functions, 8 fork calls, and 0 Echidna/Medusa/Certora harness files outside excluded deps.

scores.anchor_matched: Between 5 and 7: invariant IDs, source @custom:spec tags, and named invariant tests now exist, but the pass-status table still leaves many of 15 top-level invariants untested/partial and there is no reproducible missing-orphan script, so it remains below the 9 anchor and just short of a clean 7.

evidence.note: Canonical invariant spec defines stable IDs, machine-checkable predicates, citations, and STATED/TESTED/PROVED status semantics.
Traceability rules require @spec/INV tags; pass table shows several partial/covered rows but many IDs still lack TESTED coverage and all lack PROVED coverage.
Executable invariant suite cites INV-SALE-001/002/003/004 and INV-TOKEN-001, with function names for INV-SALE-001, INV-SALE-004, and INV-TOKEN-001.
NatSpec links sale implementation points to INV-SALE-001, INV-SALE-004, INV-SALE-002, and INV-SALE-003.
NatSpec links arbitration bond/settlement paths to INV-ARB-005, INV-ARB-004, INV-ARB-002, and INV-ARB-006.
Threat-model-to-test matrix maps several attack vectors to tests, but also records TODO gaps for A1, A10, and A12.
Observed 8 INV/spec references in test files and 13 @custom:spec/invariant-style references in src files.

scores.anchor_matched: 3 band with a small upward adjustment: coverage is above 50% and the suite is assertion-rich, but there is no mutation tool, no mutation baseline, no coverage gate, and total coverage remains below the 75% requirement for the 5 anchor.

evidence.note: Coverage completed with 256 tests passing and total coverage of 59.76% lines, 57.70% statements, 31.91% branches, and 65.34% functions.
Representative sale tests assert exact effectiveSupply, quote/ragequit payout, balances, totalSupply, and treasury deltas.
Representative arbitration tests assert exact NO bond matching and reject under-sized YES flips.
Refund/accounting behavior is checked with explicit withdrawable-balance assertions.
Main CI runs fmt, build, and forge test only; no coverage or mutation command is present.
No vertigo/gambit/sumo/mutation config and no .vertigorc/vertigo.yml were found; assertion count search showed high-assertion files but no mutation-resistance metric.

scores.anchor_matched: 5 anchor: fork tests cover major external boundaries and are env-gated, but all observed forks use floating latest blocks, CI does not set RUN_GNOSIS_FORK_TESTS, and there is no Tenderly/mainnet-shadow staging.

evidence.note: Gnosis fork test is gated by RUN_GNOSIS_FORK_TESTS and uses createSelectFork(vm.rpcUrl("gnosis")) with no block number.
End-to-end liquidity-cycle fork test is also env-gated and forks the floating Gnosis head.
Swapr/Algebra boundary test gates on RUN_GNOSIS_FORK_TESTS and forks Gnosis without a pinned block.
Main CI runs forge test without a fork-specific job or RUN_GNOSIS_FORK_TESTS setting.
Observed eight createSelectFork(vm.rpcUrl("gnosis")) call sites and no workflow match that runs fork tests with the env flag.

scores.anchor_matched: Around the 5 band: PR CI runs fmt/build/tests with a real [profile.ci], and separate Slither/symbolic workflows add gates, but Foundry/Solc are not version-pinned, coverage/gas/fork gates and deterministic seeds are absent, and forge warns that fail_on_revert is an unknown ci-profile key.

evidence.note: Main CI runs on push, pull_request, and workflow_dispatch with FOUNDRY_PROFILE=ci.
Workflow checks out code, installs foundry-rs/foundry-toolchain@v1 without a version input, then runs fmt, build, and forge test.
[profile.ci] now exists and raises fuzz/invariant runs, but line 41 uses fail_on_revert, which current Forge reports as an unknown config key.
Slither runs on PR/push paths, fails on medium/high findings, and uploads SARIF.
Nightly/manual/path-scoped PR workflow runs SMTChecker and Halmos against INV_* tests.
Local CI-profile non-fork test run passed 248/248 but emitted 'Found unknown fail_on_revert config for profile ci'.
Search found no forge coverage, forge snapshot, gas-report, codecov, deterministic seed, fork-test job, or generic uploaded test artifact in CI.

scores.anchor_matched: 7 anchor: the stack is now Foundry + Slither + SMTChecker + Halmos, and a nightly workflow invokes Halmos; it cannot reach 9 because there is only one tool from the {Echidna, Medusa, Halmos, hevm, Certora} set, no differential tests, and no committed Certora/Kontrol proof rule.

evidence.note: Slither action is wired with fail-on: medium and explicit failure re-emission.
Slither config excludes low-signal detectors/dependencies and forces Foundry compilation.
Committed profiles configure solc SMTChecker and Halmos-friendly Foundry artifacts.
CI jobs build with the smtchecker profile and install/run Halmos on invariant_INV_ tests.
No Echidna/Medusa/Certora harnesses were found outside excluded deps, and differential/equivalence search in test files returned no matches.

improvements.dimension: Layer coverage (unit / integration / fuzz / invariant / symbolic / fork)
improvements.cause: InstanceSale invariant suite plus SMTChecker/Halmos workflows add depth beyond the prior unit/integration/invariant/fork baseline.
Stable INV IDs now appear in source NatSpec and executable InstanceSale invariant tests, with a threat-model-to-test matrix.
[profile.ci], Slither PR gating, and symbolic workflows were added, though coverage/gas/version/seed/fork gates remain missing.
The stack expanded from Foundry plus Slither to include SMTChecker configuration and a scheduled Halmos workflow.

evaluator_notes: Read audit/rubrics/topic-4-sc-testing-infra.md end-to-end and compared against the prior JSONL line. Ran rubric grep/count checks, FOUNDRY_PROFILE=ci forge test --no-match-path 'test/fork/*' (248/248 passing, with unknown fail_on_revert config warning), and forge coverage --ir-minimum --report summary (256/256 passing, total line coverage 59.76%). CI/workflow scores are based on committed YAML, not live GitHub run history. Coverage total includes script/test/mocks as Forge reports it. No score-lowering caps beyond the rubric anchors were present for topic 4.
